statement.title: Statement
statement.reminder: A statement is one instruction. It almost always ends with a semicolon ;

semicolon.title: Semicolon ;
semicolon.reminder: Ends a statement. Forget one and Apex will refuse to compile.

variable-declaration.title: Variable shape
variable-declaration.reminder: TYPE name = value;  — three parts in order. Example: Integer count = 0;

type.title: Type
type.reminder: What KIND of value a variable holds. Apex requires you to declare the type.

string-quotes.title: String quotes
string-quotes.reminder: Apex strings use SINGLE quotes only — 'hello', not "hello".

camel-case.title: camelCase
camel-case.reminder: Variables and methods: lowercase first word, capitalize each next word. myAge, isLearning.

pascal-case.title: PascalCase
pascal-case.reminder: Types and class names: capitalize EVERY word, including the first. Integer, Account, MyHelper.

case-insensitive.title: Apex is case-insensitive
case-insensitive.reminder: myVariable and MyVariable are the same to the compiler — but pick one style and be consistent.

block.title: Block { }
block.reminder: Curly braces group code that runs together — class bodies, method bodies, if-blocks, loops.

assignment-operator.title: = is assignment
assignment-operator.reminder: A single = puts the value on the right INTO the variable on the left. == is comparison (later).

string-concat.title: + joins strings
string-concat.reminder: Use + to concatenate strings: 'Hello, ' + name. Same operator that adds numbers.

method-shape.title: Method anatomy
method-shape.reminder: modifier static? returnType name(params) { body } — e.g. public static void doIt() { ... }

void.title: void
void.reminder: A method whose return type is void returns nothing — it just runs.

public-modifier.title: public
public-modifier.reminder: An access modifier — public means any other class can use this. Alternatives: private, protected.

static-vs-instance.title: static vs instance
static-vs-instance.reminder: static = belongs to the class itself, called as ClassName.method(). Instance = belongs to one object, called as obj.method().

parameter-vs-argument.title: Parameter vs Argument
parameter-vs-argument.reminder: Parameter = the named slot in the method definition. Argument = the actual value you pass when calling.

class-vs-object.title: Class vs Object
class-vs-object.reminder: Class = the blueprint. Object (instance) = one actual thing built from it.

decimal-vs-double.title: Decimal vs Double
decimal-vs-double.reminder: Money → Decimal (exact). Decimal preserves precision; Double approximates and accumulates rounding error.

list-collection.title: List
list-collection.reminder: Ordered, indexed, allows duplicates. Like a Spotify playlist. Access by [index], starting at 0.

set-collection.title: Set
set-collection.reminder: Unordered, unique values. Adding a duplicate is silently ignored. Use for dedup.

map-collection.title: Map
map-collection.reminder: Key → value pairs. Look up by key with .get(key). Like a phone book.

new-keyword.title: new keyword
new-keyword.reminder: 'new ClassName(...)' creates a fresh instance. Used for collections (new List<...>()) and your own classes.

for-loop.title: for-loop
for-loop.reminder: for (Integer i = 0; i < N; i++) { } — counter loop. Three parts: init, condition, advance.

for-each-loop.title: for-each loop
for-each-loop.reminder: for (Type item : collection) { } — walks every item in a collection.

while-loop.title: while-loop
while-loop.reminder: while (condition) { } — runs as long as condition stays true. Add a safety counter to avoid infinite loops.

soql-for-loop.title: SOQL for-loop
soql-for-loop.reminder: for (Account a : [SELECT ...]) { } streams results in chunks of 200 — safe for huge result sets.

continue.title: continue
continue.reminder: Skips to the next iteration of the loop, leaving the current one unfinished.

return-statement.title: return
return-statement.reminder: Ends the method and ships a value back to the caller. void methods may have no return at all.

private-modifier.title: private
private-modifier.reminder: Access modifier — only the same class can call this method or read this field. Use to hide internals.

auto-property.title: Auto-property
auto-property.reminder: { get; set; } shorthand — Apex auto-generates trivial getter and setter.

custom-getter.title: Custom getter
custom-getter.reminder: get { return ... } — runs your code when something reads the property.

custom-setter.title: Custom setter
custom-setter.reminder: set { ... = value; } — 'value' is the magic name for the incoming assignment.

this-keyword.title: this
this-keyword.reminder: Refers to the current instance. Use it to disambiguate when a parameter shares a name with a field: this.id = id;

soql.title: SOQL
soql.reminder: [SELECT field FROM Object WHERE ... LIMIT n] — Apex's database query syntax. Always inside square brackets.

where-clause.title: WHERE clause
where-clause.reminder: Filters SOQL results. Operators: = != > < >= <= LIKE IN. Combine with AND / OR.

soql-limit.title: LIMIT
soql-limit.reminder: Caps the number of rows a SOQL query returns. Always include one when you don't need every row.

soql-bind-variable.title: Bind variable
soql-bind-variable.reminder: Use :myVar to inject an Apex value into SOQL. NEVER concatenate strings into a query — that's SOQL injection.

relationship-query.title: Relationship query
relationship-query.reminder: Walk parent→child via subquery: SELECT (SELECT Email FROM Contacts) FROM Account. Walk child→parent via dot: SELECT Account.Name FROM Contact.

dml-insert.title: insert
dml-insert.reminder: DML verb — creates new records and stamps Ids onto your Apex variable.

dml-update.title: update
dml-update.reminder: DML verb — modifies existing records (must already have an Id).

dml-upsert.title: upsert
dml-upsert.reminder: DML verb — insert OR update based on whether the record matches an Id (or external Id).

dml-delete.title: delete
dml-delete.reminder: DML verb — soft-deletes records to the Recycle Bin. undelete brings them back.

bulk-dml.title: Bulk DML
bulk-dml.reminder: Always DML on a List, not one record at a time. ONE 'insert listOfLeads;' = ONE DML statement (not N).

database-class.title: Database class
database-class.reminder: Database.insert(records, allOrNone=false) returns SaveResults — partial-success DML for imports.

all-or-none.title: allOrNone
all-or-none.reminder: Database DML option. true (default) = whole batch fails on any error. false = save what's valid, return errors per failure.

trigger.title: Trigger
trigger.reminder: trigger Name on Object (events) { } — Apex code that fires on database events.

trigger-event.title: Trigger events
trigger-event.reminder: before/after × insert/update/delete (+ after undelete). Choose based on whether you need the record's Id and whether you want free DML.

trigger-context.title: Trigger context
trigger-context.reminder: Trigger.new (list of new versions), Trigger.old (old versions), Trigger.newMap / oldMap (keyed by Id), Trigger.isInsert / isBefore / etc.

trigger-handler-pattern.title: Trigger handler pattern
trigger-handler-pattern.reminder: Best practice: thin trigger delegates ALL logic to a regular Apex class for testability.

test-annotation.title: @isTest
test-annotation.reminder: Marks a class or method as test-only. Test classes don't count toward your code limit.

assert.title: System.assert
assert.reminder: System.assert(condition, message) — fails the test if the condition is false.

assert-equals.title: System.assertEquals
assert-equals.reminder: System.assertEquals(expected, actual, message) — fails if values differ.

test-startStop.title: Test.startTest / stopTest
test-startStop.reminder: Wrap code under test. Resets governor limits at start; forces async to flush at stop.

test-setup.title: @TestSetup
test-setup.reminder: Method that runs once before each test method in the class. Use for shared test data.

try-catch.title: try / catch
try-catch.reminder: try { risky } catch (ExceptionType e) { handle }. Only catch exceptions you can actually handle.

future-annotation.title: @future
future-annotation.reminder: Marks a static void method to run async in a separate transaction. Params must be primitives or collections of primitives.

interfaces.title: Interface
interfaces.reminder: Pure contract — only method signatures. Classes 'implements' an interface and provide bodies.

extends-keyword.title: extends
extends-keyword.reminder: 'class B extends A' — B inherits A's properties and methods. Single inheritance only in Apex.

virtual-keyword.title: virtual
virtual-keyword.reminder: Modifier on a parent class or method that opts in to being subclassed/overridden.

override-keyword.title: override
override-keyword.reminder: Modifier on a child method explicitly replacing the parent's behavior. Required, not optional.

abstract-keyword.title: abstract
abstract-keyword.reminder: abstract class can't be instantiated; abstract method has no body and must be overridden by subclasses.

named-credential.title: Named Credential
named-credential.reminder: Reference 'callout:Name/path' instead of a raw URL. Salesforce expands it and injects auth — no hardcoded secrets.

rest-resource.title: @RestResource
rest-resource.reminder: @RestResource(urlMapping='/path/*') exposes an Apex class as a REST endpoint at /services/apexrest/path.

http-method-annotation.title: @HttpGet / @HttpPost
http-method-annotation.reminder: Method annotations inside an @RestResource class — route a verb to a handler method.

ttl.title: TTL
ttl.reminder: Time To Live — how long a cache entry remains valid before it expires.

aura-enabled-cacheable.title: @AuraEnabled(cacheable=true)
aura-enabled-cacheable.reminder: LWC-callable read-only methods marked cacheable=true are cached on the client per-input.
